metadata.credit: Data and analysis provided by Draftsim
metadata.author: Bryan Hohns (Veveil)

set_overview.summary: Khans of Tarkir emphasizes multicolored cards and the morph mechanic. The format supports 4-5 color decks with heavy land drafting. Compared to modern sets the number of outright bad cards is significantly higher, making card quality evaluation crucial. Khans rewards bombs + mana fixing more than typical Limited formats due to the slow environment and abundant land availability.
color_ranking: {W}{B}{G} Abzan — contains the best 3-color cards in the set
{U}{R}{W} Jeskai — splashy 3-color cards with strong tempo
{R}{W}{B} Mardu — aggressive with immediate damage potential
{G}{U}{R} Temur — big creatures well above rate; smooth starts required

color_notes.Note: KTK is clan-driven (3-color wedges). Most decks should start as 5c/good-stuff and migrate into a clan as signals dictate.

key_gameplay_notes: Bombs + mana fixing matter more than in modern formats.
Slow format — strong cards are dominant; weak cards drag decks down hard.
Morphs smooth mana requirements and slow game pace.
Removal is notably weaker than modern standards; instants are often playable without response windows.
The Rule of 5: most morphs with strong combat stats cost 5+ mana to flip — large threats won't suddenly appear if your opponent lacks 5 mana.

mechanics.name: Outlast (Abzan)
mechanics.summary: Pay mana and tap to put a +1/+1 counter on this creature (sorcery speed).
mechanics.performance: Long-game payoff for Abzan; pairs with counter lords.

mechanics.name: Prowess (Jeskai)
mechanics.summary: Whenever you cast a noncreature spell, this creature gets +1/+1 until end of turn.
mechanics.performance: Pairs cheap creatures with instants/sorceries for tempo.

mechanics.name: Delve (Sultai)
mechanics.summary: Exile cards from your graveyard to reduce the cost of this spell.
mechanics.performance: Only play 1-2 delve cards or build specifically around the mechanic.

mechanics.name: Raid (Mardu)
mechanics.summary: Trigger when you have attacked with a creature this turn.

mechanics.name: Ferocious (Temur)
mechanics.summary: Triggers when you control a creature with power 4 or greater.
mechanics.performance: Rewards big creatures; struggles from behind.

mechanics.name: Morph
mechanics.summary: Cast any creature with morph face-down as a 2/2 for 3 mana, then pay the morph cost to flip it face-up. Morphing does not use the stack — opponents get no response window.
mechanics.performance: Format-defining. Smooths mana, slows the game, and adds bluffing.

tier_1.name: Abzan Outlast
tier_1.strategy: Long-game approach with counter payoffs. Stack outlast lords; multicolor cards are essential for premium removal.

tier_2.name: Jeskai Prowess
tier_2.strategy: Tempo decks mixing cheap creatures with instant/sorcery spells. Aggressive with fliers and Arrow Storm; Jeskai Ascendancy is better in a tempo shell.

tier_2.name: Mardu Raid
tier_2.strategy: Most aggressive clan; rarely plays slowly. Wide-based aggression with immediate damage potential.

tier_2.name: Temur Ferocious
tier_2.strategy: Creature-heavy beatdown that maximizes 4/x power triggers. Struggles from behind — needs smooth starts.

tier_3.name: Sultai Delve
tier_3.strategy: Grindy, graveyard-focused control. Watch for decking; fliers (Sultai Scavenger) provide inevitability.

bwr_warriors.description: Lean curve punishing 5-color decks. Ponyback Brigade is the primary reason for the third color. Can function as 2-color (WB) or full Mardu.

ug_bear_punch.description: Savage Punch + Alpine Grizzly for rapid pressure. Punishes greedy mana bases by exploiting fast clocks.

ugx_morphs.description: Build around Secret Plans and Trail of Mystery. Best uncommon morphs (Dragon's Eye Savants, Temur Charger) reveal-trigger value.

toughness_matters.description: Limited payoffs; only Kin-Tree Invocation is substantial. Support with Disowned Ancestor.

draft_priority: Premium removal (Debilitating Injury, Bring Low, Kill Shot)
Card draw (Bitter Revelation, Treasure Cruise, Weave Fate)
Quality cards (top commons, efficient uncommons, strong morphs)
Mediocre removal (Rite of the Serpent, Throttle)
Weak commons (Sagu Archer, Archers' Parapet, Rotting Mastodon)

mana_base_guidance.land_counts: 17-19 lands standard; 16 acceptable for Warriors aggro; 15-16 possible for fast red (not recommended).
mana_base_guidance.color_distribution: 8+ lands for primary colors; 3-4 lands for a 1-2 card splash.
mana_base_guidance.taplands: Acceptable at 4-7 copies for 3-color decks; 5-color piles can handle more. Keep 6-8 basics for early plays.
mana_base_guidance.sequencing_example: Tapland t1 → tapland t2 → untapped t3 (play morph) → tapland t4 → untapped t5 (flip morph).
mana_base_guidance.warning: Don't play off-color taplands for marginal life gain.

general_tips: Position yourself for a 5c/good-stuff pile early on, then move into a specific clan if you keep getting passed good cards.
Take relevant dual lands over just about any common.
Morphs function as mana sinks reducing flood impact.
Power creep changes mean modern drafters should expect KTK to feel somewhat awkward compared to contemporary sets.
